title: Your Official English Book Details
simple_explanation: This page is like the official ID card for your English book. It has all the important names and titles to show you it's the right one for your learning this year!
detailed_explanation: It clearly states the book's name, edition, and the primary authors and series editors. This ensures you know exactly which version of the textbook you are using. You'll also see the publisher's name at the bottom, which is the company that produced and printed your book. It's like seeing the brand name on your favourite toy.

examples_expanded.text: You can see 'Marie Lallaway' as an author and 'Christine Chen' and 'Lindsay Pickton' as series editors, just like how a toy might list the designer and the company that made it.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of identifying your textbook information in a real sentence.

guided_practice: Identify the meaning of 'Publisher' in a sentence. Remember it means: A company or person that prepares and issues books, journals, or music for sale.
Look at the first example again. How would you modify it to make it your own?
application_tasks: Create 3 of your own sentences using the core idea of Identifying Your Textbook Information.
Imagine you are teaching this topic to a friend. Write down exactly what you would say.
challenge_tasks: Combine the concept of 'Publisher' with something you learned in a previous chapter to write a short paragraph.
Can you find an exception to this rule or a creative way to bend it? Explain how.
key_points: This page officially confirms the title, edition, and authors of your textbook.
It helps you identify the specific book you'll be using for your English lessons.
Knowing the edition (like 'Second Edition') is useful as textbooks sometimes get updated with new content.

vocabulary.definition: A company or person that prepares and issues books, journals, or music for sale.
